content:
Default: **modular monolith + single PostgreSQL database**.

Why:
- **Fastest to ship** for a 6-person team with limited DevOps.
- **Lowest operational complexity** before first release.
- Fits **modest initial traffic** easily.
- Keeps **billing and core app** separated by code/module boundaries so you can split later if needed.

Guardrails:
- Enforce clear module boundaries now.
- Avoid cross-module table coupling where possible.
- Use one deployable, one DB, one migration path.
- Revisit service split only after real load or org needs appear.

High risk: `req.headers['x-user-id']` is client-controlled, so any caller can impersonate another user by setting that header. This is an auth bypass / privilege escalation if `userId` is used for authorization or data access.

Verify:
- Send a request with `x-user-id` for a different account and confirm access is denied.
- Trace all uses of `userId` to ensure none trust the header.
- Remove the header fallback; derive identity only from verified session/token claims.

@sigil v0 hybrid
G: fix(auth_middleware_boundary_expiry_skew_tolerance)
C: bc_preserved ∧ sec_preserved ∧ smallest_diff ∧ add_1_regression_test ∧ accept_30s_clock_skew ∧ no_past_expiry_acceptance
H: exp_check_uses_strict_lt_without_skew
P: "constSKEW_MS=30000" → "if(expMs+SKEW_MS<nowMs){return res.status(401).json({error:'expired_token'})}"
V: token_exp_eq_now_minus_30s_accepts ∧ token_exp_lt_now_minus_30s_401
R: ! over_accept_if_skew_applied_wrong_side ∧ ! test_time_flakiness
A: add_30s_skew_to_expiry_check_and_regress_boundary_case

[AUDIT]
Fix auth_middleware_boundary_expiry_skew_tolerance, preserve bc_preserved, sec_preserved, and verify with token_exp_eq_now_minus_30s_accepts.

@sigil v0 hybrid
G: decide(modular_monolith_on_PostgreSQL_with_clear_domain_boundaries)
C: team_6 ∧ part_time_devops_generalist ∧ "deadline_4 months" ∧ modest_initial_traffic ∧ single_PostgreSQL ∧ no_pre_release_split ∧ future_split_billing_core_app_post_release
H: fastest_to_ship ∧ lowest_operational_complexity ∧ fits_small_team ∧ keeps_transactional_consistency ∧ eases_later_extraction_via_module_boundaries ⇒ prefer(modular_monolith_on_PostgreSQL_with_clear_domain_boundaries)
P: start_monolith → enforce_bounded_contexts → single_db → design_for_extractability → defer_service_split_until_real_pressure
R: ! module_leakage ∧ ! coupled_schema ∧ ! over_abstracting_too_early ∧ ! future_split_cost_if_boundaries_sloppy
A: modular_monolith_on_PostgreSQL ∧ split_later_if_needed

[AUDIT]
Choose modular_monolith_on_PostgreSQL_with_clear_domain_boundaries under team_6, part_time_devops_generalist and keep the path to future decomposition open.

@sigil v0 hybrid
G: review(patch)
H: client_supplied_x-user-id_overrides_authenticated_session_identity ⇒ send_x-user-id_in_request_to_impersonate_another_user_when_header_is_trusted_before_session_fallback
P: ignore_x-user-id_for_identity → derive_user_id_only_from_verified_session_or_signed_token → if_header_needed_for_internal_calls_require_gateway_strip_and_service_auth
V: request_with_x-user-id_and_valid_session_returns_header_value_instead_of_session_user_id ∧ request_with_spoofed_x-user-id_cannot_access_another_user
R: ! authz_bypass ∧ ! account_impersonation ∧ ! data_exposure
A: critical_id_trust_regression

[AUDIT]
Treat client_supplied_x-user-id_overrides_authenticated_session_identity as a trust-boundary issue and apply ignore_x-user-id_for_identity before merge.

@sigil v0 hybrid
G: refactor(loadUser_async_await_minimal_change)
C: "preserve_validation_order: missing_id -> db.findUser -> not_found -> audit.log -> cache.set" ∧ "single_db_error_mapping_site: db.findUser err => next(err)" ∧ preserve_next_signatures_and_outcomes ∧ no_behavior_change_for_audit_or_cache_error_paths
P: wrap_loadUser_in_async_function → "await db.findUser(id) with try/catch around db_only" → "keep missing_id check before await" → "keep not_found check immediately after db result" → "await audit.log(user.id) and cache.set(user.id,user) via promisify_or_inline_promise_wrapper"
V: "id_absent => next(Error('missing_id'))" ∧ "db_err => next(err) only once" ∧ "no_user => next(Error('not_found'))" ∧ "audit_err => next(logErr)" ∧ "cache_err => next(cacheErr)" ∧ "success => next(null, user)"
R: ! promisify_wrappers_may_change_this_binding ∧ ! accidental_reordering_of_checks ∧ ! double_error_mapping ∧ ! callback_api_mismatch_for_audit_or_cache
A: convert_to_async_await_keep_callbacks

[AUDIT]
Refactor loadUser_async_await_minimal_change while preserving "preserve_validation_order: missing_id -> db.findUser -> not_found -> audit.log -> cache.set", "single_db_error_mapping_site: db.findUser err => next(err)".

c: preserve_validation_order: missing_id -> db.findUser -> not_found -> audit.log -> cache.set
p: await db.findUser(id) with try/catch around db_only
keep not_found check immediately after db result
await audit.log(user.id) and cache.set(user.id,user) via promisify_or_inline_promise_wrapper
